original_abstract: This paper explores the educational philosophy of Imam Al-Ghazali, specifically as articulated in his seminal work, *Ihya’ Ulumiddin*, and examines its enduring relevance in the contemporary pedagogical landscape. A towering figure in the Islamic tradition, Al-Ghazali argued that education should transcend the simple transfer of information, serving instead as a transformative process for shaping an individual’s character, ethics, and spiritual depth. Utilizing a library research methodology, this study analyzes Al-Ghazali’s core principles regarding moral and ethical development to evaluate how these traditional views can address the complex challenges of modern schooling. Al-Ghazali championed learning methods that are active, reflective, and anchored in spiritual values—models that offer a robust framework for strengthening character education today. Ultimately, this research seeks to bridge the gap between classical Islamic thought and modern educational needs, identifying how Al-Ghazali's holistic vision can contribute to a more balanced and ethically grounded global education system.
humanized_abstract: This paper explores the educational philosophy of Imam Al-Ghazali as outlined in his monumental book Ihya’ Ulumiddin. It aims at validating its applicability to contemporary educational practices. Al-Ghazali, a unique scholar in the Islamic civilization, believed that education is not merely a process of transmitting knowledge but rather developing the learner’s inner self to achieve a complete human development. This study adopted the library research approach. It reviewed and discussed Al-Ghazali’s views on the basic elements of moral and ethical development in man and verified their relevance to contemporary challenges in the educational system. The study concludes that Al-Ghazali’s educational ideology, which relies on the activation, reflection and the elevation of educational processes to the level of spiritual education, provides sound references for strengthening the character building process in contemporary schools. The study aims at narrowing the gap between Islamic educational thoughts and contemporary educational needs. It endeavours to prove that Imam Al-Ghazali’s educational ideology, which is based on a comprehensive education that builds a balanced human personality, is applicable to the contemporary global educational system.
